content.problemStatement:
Chef is participating in a pogo stick race. In this competition, there are NNN squares (numbered 111 through NNN) in a row. Chef must choose a starting square, enter this square and start jumping on his pogo stick. In each jump, if Chef is at a square sss, then he can only jump to the square s+Ks+Ks+K. If square s+Ks+Ks+K does not exist, Chef jumps out of the row of squares and the race ends for him. It is not allowed to stop jumping any earlier.

Each square has a value; let's denote the value of the iii-th square by AiA_iAi​. Initially, Chef has 000 points. When he jumps in some square (including the initial square), he is awarded a number of points equal to the value of this square, exactly once. Note that if this value is negative, the number of Chef's points is decreased.

Determine the maximum possible total number of points Chef can get if he selects the initial cell optimally.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10,00010,00010,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the maximum number of points.
